task: I’m drafting a short reply to a seller who just revised the terms after we were already moving toward a deal. I want to keep the message brief and professional while making the next step clear.

turns.agent_response:
Here’s a tight version you can use:

[Seller Name],

Thanks for the update. We’re not able to accept the revised terms. Please send over your best and final position, and we’ll decide next steps from there.

[Your Name]

If you want, I can make it firmer or even shorter.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but make it a little firmer. I’d want the seller to feel the friction without me sounding emotional.

Something like:

[Seller Name],

We were already moving forward under the prior terms, so we’re not prepared to proceed on the revised version. If you want to keep this active, send your best and final position and we’ll review it.

[Your Name]

That’s cleaner. Less chatty.

turns.agent_response:
That’s strong. If you want it just a touch firmer and tighter, I’d use:

[Seller Name],

We had been moving forward on the prior terms, so we’re not prepared to proceed on the revised version. If you want to keep this active, send your best and final position and we’ll review it.

[Your Name]

If you want, I can make it even colder or more direct.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That works.

It’s firm without getting theatrical, and it leaves the ball in their court.

I’d send it as-is.
